name: Scotch Black Bun
recipeInstructions:
1. The butter should be cut into small pieces.
2. Combine the butter with 3 3/4 cups flour and 1/2 teaspoon salt in a medium-sized mixing bowl. Mix until small granules form, then sprinkle 8 tablespoons ice water over the mixture. Continue mixing until a soft ball can be formed. If needed, add more ice water to achieve a soft dough. Press the dough into a thick cake, wrap it in plastic wrap, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.
3. In a large bowl, sift together 3 1/3 cups flour, sugar, baking powder, spices (including black pepper), and remaining salt. Add the ground black pepper, raisins (seedless and white or golden), ground almonds, and coarsely chopped almonds. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly coated.
4. In a separate bowl, beat 3 eggs and mix in buttermilk and brandy until combined.
5. Preheat the oven to 375 degrees F (190 degrees C).
6. Roll out two-thirds of the chilled pastry dough to a thickness of 1/4 inch on a lightly floured surface. Place the rolled-out dough into a 9 x 3 1/2-inch springform pan, forming a disk by cutting along the outside of the pan.
7. Line the bottom of the pan with the dough disk and use remaining pastry pieces to line the sides of the pan, slightly overlapping the dough on the bottom and extending about 1/2 inch above the top of the form.
8. Spoon the filling into the pan, packing it firmly and pressing excess dough over the filling. Brush the dough lightly with beaten egg.
9. Roll out the remaining pastry dough to a circle larger than the pan, then roll around a rolling pin to lift and unroll onto the top of the form. Press the top crust against the filling and the bottom crust, creating a tight seal and trimming excess dough.
10. Brush the entire top with beaten egg. Cut a 1-inch round vent in the center and use pastry scraps to create leaf shapes if desired. Roll the scraps into leaves and cut out tear drop shapes, then score the leaves with a sharp knife to resemble ribs.
11. Brush undersides of the leaves with remaining beaten egg for adhesion, then brush the top of the bun again with beaten egg.
12. Bake on a rack in the middle of the oven for 2 to 2 1/2 hours, or until the pastry is a rich golden brown. If decorations brown faster than the pastry top, cover them with aluminum foil to reflect heat and prevent further browning.
